seoTitle: How to Use Multiple Slack Accounts at Once (2026)
metaDescription: Add and switch between multiple Slack workspaces in one desktop app using the workspace switcher and quick keyboard shortcuts.
intro: Juggling separate client servers or multiple company workspaces gets messy without a proper multi-account setup. This guide shows how to add additional Slack workspaces to your desktop app and switch between them instantly.

steps.heading: Open the workspace switcher
steps.body: Click the plus icon or your current workspace logo in the top left of the Slack desktop app.

steps.heading: Add a new workspace
steps.body: Select 'Add a workspace' from the dropdown to start the account linking process.

steps.heading: Sign in to another workspace
steps.body: Choose 'Sign in to another workspace', which opens your browser to complete authentication.

steps.heading: Verify your identity
steps.body: Enter your email or workspace URL and confirm the sign-in with the 6-digit code sent to you.

steps.heading: Switch between workspaces
steps.body: Click the newly added workspace icon on the left rail, or use Ctrl/Cmd plus a number key to jump between accounts quickly.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many workspaces I can add?
faq.answer: Slack doesn't impose a strict limit on the number of workspaces you can connect to a single desktop app.

faq.question: Can I mute notifications for one workspace only?
faq.answer: Yes, each workspace has its own notification settings you can adjust independently.

faq.question: Do I need a different email for each workspace?
faq.answer: No, you can use the same email across multiple workspaces as long as you were invited or signed up separately for each.

faq.question: Does this work the same way on mobile?
faq.answer: Yes, the Slack mobile app also supports adding and switching between multiple workspaces.
